assertion_results.text: Tasks target both 'trustify-backend' and 'trustify-ui' repositories with at least one task per repo
assertion_results.evidence: Tasks 2 and 3 target trustify-backend (Repository: trustify-backend). Tasks 1, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8 target trustify-ui (Repository: trustify-ui). Both repos have at least one task.

assertion_results.text: Backend tasks have lower task numbers than the frontend tasks that depend on them
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks are Task 2 (model and service) and Task 3 (endpoint). Frontend tasks that depend on them start at Task 4. Task 4 explicitly depends on Task 3. All backend task numbers (2, 3) are lower than all frontend task numbers (4, 5, 6, 7).

assertion_results.text: UI-facing frontend tasks (pages, components) reference Figma design context mentioning specific PatternFly components and visual specifications — API-layer frontend tasks (API types, client functions, hooks) are exempt from this requirement
assertion_results.evidence: Task 5 (SbomComparePage, UI-facing) extensively references PatternFly components: Select (single, typeahead), ExpandableSection, Badge with colors (green/red/blue/yellow), Table (composable), EmptyState with CodeBranchIcon, Skeleton, Dropdown, Button, and SeverityBadge. Task 6 (SbomListPage selection, UI-facing) references PatternFly Table row selection and Button. Task 4 (API types, client function, hook) is API-layer and exempt. Task 7 (E2E test) is not a UI component task.

assertion_results.text: Cross-repo dependency ordering is enforced: either at least one frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task in its Dependencies section, or all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Task 4 (frontend) explicitly depends on Task 3 (backend): 'Depends on: Task 3 — Add GET /api/v2/sbom/compare endpoint (API contract must be finalized)'. Additionally, all intermediate tasks target the shared feature branch TC-9003 and backend tasks (2, 3) have lower numbers than frontend tasks (4, 5, 6, 7).

assertion_results.text: Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md; backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md
assertion_results.evidence: Backend Task 2 references paths from repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/comparison.rs (new file following established pattern in model/), modules/fundamental/src/package/model/summary.rs, modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs. Backend Task 3 references: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, tests/api/sbom_compare.rs. Frontend Task 4 references: src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts. Frontend Task 5 references: src/routes.tsx, src/pages/SbomComparePage/*, src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, src/components/FilterToolbar.tsx. Frontend Task 6 references: src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx. Frontend Task 7 references: tests/e2e/sbom-compare.spec.ts.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 tasks have a '## Repository' section with exactly one repo name: Task 1: trustify-ui, Task 2: trustify-backend, Task 3: trustify-backend, Task 4: trustify-ui, Task 5: trustify-ui, Task 6: trustify-ui, Task 7: trustify-ui, Task 8: trustify-ui.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 tasks contain: ## Target Branch (Task 1: main, Tasks 2-7: TC-9003, Task 8: main), ## Description, ## Acceptance Criteria (with checkbox items), ## Test Requirements (with checkbox items). Verified in all task files.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the corresponding mock repository structure manifests (repo-backend.md or repo-frontend.md), not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Backend Task 2 Files to Modify: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (in repo-backend.md), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (in repo-backend.md). Files to Create: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/comparison.rs (new file in existing directory modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/ from repo-backend.md). Backend Task 3 Files to Modify: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (in repo-backend.md). Files to Create: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/compare.rs (new file in existing endpoints/ dir), tests/api/sbom_compare.rs (new file in tests/api/ from repo-backend.md). Frontend Task 4 Files to Modify: src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts (both in repo-frontend.md). Files to Create: src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts (new file in existing hooks/ dir). Frontend Task 5 Files to Modify: src/routes.tsx (in repo-frontend.md). Files to Create: src/pages/SbomComparePage/*.tsx (new page following the page directory pattern). Frontend Task 6 Files to Modify: src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx (in repo-frontend.md). All paths are consistent with the repo structure manifests.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-bookend task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance — bookend tasks (create-branch, merge-branch) are exempt as they omit Implementation Notes by design
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 (create-branch bookend) and Task 8 (merge-branch bookend) omit Implementation Notes — correct per design. Task 2 references specific paths: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/ pattern with summary.rs and details.rs, SbomService in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, PackageSummary from modules/fundamental/src/package/model/summary.rs, AdvisorySummary from modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs, entity/src/sbom_package.rs, entity/src/sbom_advisory.rs. Task 3 references: endpoint pattern in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/ with list.rs and get.rs, route registration in mod.rs, tests/api/sbom.rs pattern. Task 4 references: src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts with fetchSboms() pattern, src/hooks/useSboms.ts and useSbomById.ts patterns, src/api/client.ts. Task 5 references: src/pages/SbomDetailPage/ structure, useSboms hook, FilterToolbar, EmptyStateCard, LoadingSpinner, SeverityBadge.tsx, severityUtils.ts, PackageTable.tsx, AdvisoryList.tsx. Task 6 references: SbomListPage.tsx, FilterToolbar.tsx, useNavigate(). Task 7 references: tests/e2e/sbom-list.spec.ts, tests/setup.ts, tests/mocks/handlers.ts.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section — either all set to 'main' (direct-to-main workflow) or bookend tasks set to 'main' with intermediate tasks set to the feature issue ID (feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Feature-branch workflow is used. Bookend tasks (Task 1 create-branch and Task 8 merge-branch) have Target Branch: main. All intermediate tasks (Tasks 2-7) have Target Branch: TC-9003 (the feature issue ID). This is consistent feature-branch workflow.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: No digest comments were found in any of the 8 output task files. Searched for 'sha256', 'digest', and 'sdlc-workflow' across all output files — no matches. The eval outputs do not contain any description digest comments.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No convention references were found in any output files. Searched for 'CONVENTIONS', 'convention', 'Per CONVENTIONS', and 'Applies:' across all output files — no matches. There is no evidence of convention-aware enrichment in any task's Implementation Notes.
